title: विष्णु पञ्जर स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विष्णु पञ्जर स्तोत्रम् ॥

नारदोवाच —
भगवन् सर्वधर्मज्ञ सर्वशास्त्रविशारद।
विष्णोः पञ्जरमाख्याहि येन स्यात् सुखमुत्तमम्॥ १॥

भगवानुवाच —
शृणु वत्स प्रवक्ष्यामि विष्णोः पञ्जरमुत्तमम्।
यस्य स्मरणमात्रेण सर्वसिद्धिर्भवेन्नृणाम्॥ २॥

ॐ विष्णवे नमः शिरः पातु केशवो ललाटकम्।
नारायणो दृशौ पातु श्रीधरः पातु नासिकाम्॥ ३॥

ओष्ठौ पातु गदाधरः श्रीवत्साङ्कः स्तनान्तरम्।
मधुसूदनो हृदि स्थितो मध्यं पातु जनार्दनः॥ ४॥

नाभिं पद्मनिधिः पातु कटिं वैकुण्ठनायकः।
ऊरू पातु हृषीकेशो जानुनी मधुसूदनः॥ ५॥

जङ्घे पातु हरिः साक्षात् पादौ पातु त्रिविक्रमः।
सर्वाङ्गं चक्रपाणिस्तु सर्वदा परिरक्षतु॥ ६॥

पूर्वस्यां पुण्डरीकाक्षः आग्नेय्यां श्रीधरोऽवतु।
दक्षिणे वामनः पातु नैर्ऋत्यां माधवोऽवतु॥ ७॥

पश्चिमे पातु गोविन्दो वायव्यां पातु केशवः।
उदीच्यां पातु विश्वेशो ह्यैशान्यां पातु ईश्वरः॥ ८॥

ऊर्ध्वं पातु गजारूढः अधः पातु सनातनः।
एवं दशदिशो रक्षेद्विष्णुः प्रभुरनन्तकः॥ ९॥

वज्रपञ्जरनामेदं यो विष्णोः कवचं पठेत्।
तस्य शत्रुक्षयो नित्यं विजयश्चैव जायते॥ १०॥

सर्वमङ्गलमाङ्गल्यं सर्वपापप्रणाशनम्।
सर्वरोगप्रशमनं विष्णोः पञ्जरमुत्तमम्॥ ११॥

॥ इति श्री विष्णु पञ्जर स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vishnu Panjara Stotram ||

Naradovacha —
Bhagavan sarva-dharmajna sarva-shastra-visharada.
Vishnoh panjaram-akhyahi yena syat sukham-uttamam. || 1 ||

Bhagavan-uvacha —
Shrinu vatsa pravakshyami Vishnoh panjaram-uttamam.
Yasya smarana-matrena sarva-siddhir-bhaven-nrinam. || 2 ||

Om Vishnave namah shirah patu Keshavo lalaatakam.
Narayano drishow patu Shridharah patu nasikam. || 3 ||

Oshthau patu Gadadharah Shrivatsa-ankah stanantaram.
Madhusudano hridi sthito madhyam patu Janardanah. || 4 ||

Nabhim Padma-nidhih patu katim Vaikuntha-nayakah.
Uru patu Hrishikesho januni Madhusudanah. || 5 ||

Janghe patu Harih sakshat padau patu Trivikramah.
Sarvangam Chakra-panis-tu sarvada parirakshatu. || 6 ||

Purvasyam Pundarikakshah agneyam Shridharo-avatu.
Dakshine Vamanah patu nairityam Madhavo-avatu. || 7 ||

Pashchime patu Govindo vayavyam patu Keshavah.
Udichyam patu Vishvesho hyaishanayam patu Ishvarah. || 8 ||

Urdhvam patu Gajarudhah adhah patu Sanatanah.
Evam dasha-disho rakshed-Vishnuh prabhur-anantakah. || 9 ||

Vajra-panjara-namedam yo Vishnoh kavacham pathet.
Tasya shatru-kshayo nityam vijayash-chaiva jayate. || 10 ||

Sarva-mangala-mangalyam sarva-papa-pranashanam.
Sarva-roga-prashamanam Vishnoh panjaram-uttamam. || 11 ||

|| Iti Shri Vishnu Panjara Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
नारद ने कहा — हे भगवन्! सर्व धर्मज्ञ, सर्व शास्त्र विशारद! विष्णु का पञ्जर (रक्षा कवच) बताइए जिससे उत्तम सुख की प्राप्ति हो।

भगवान ने कहा — हे वत्स! सुनो, मैं विष्णु का उत्तम पञ्जर बताता हूँ, जिसके स्मरण मात्र से मनुष्यों को सर्व सिद्धि प्राप्त होती है।

ॐ विष्णु को नमस्कार। शिर की केशव रक्षा करें, ललाट की नारायण, नेत्रों की श्रीधर, नासिका की रक्षा करें। ओठों की गदाधर, वक्षस्थल की श्रीवत्सांक, हृदय में मधुसूदन स्थित हों, मध्य भाग की जनार्दन रक्षा करें।

नाभि की पद्मनिधि, कटि की वैकुण्ठनायक, ऊरु की हृषीकेश, घुटनों की मधुसूदन, जंघाओं की साक्षात् हरि, पैरों की त्रिविक्रम और सम्पूर्ण अंगों की चक्रपाणि सदा रक्षा करें।

दसों दिशाओं में विष्णु भगवान रक्षा करें। इस वज्र पञ्जर नाम के कवच को पढ़ने वाले के शत्रुओं का नाश और सदा विजय होती है। यह सर्व मंगलकारी, सर्व पापनाशक और सर्व रोगनाशक है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीविष्णुपञ्जरस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य नारद ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्री विष्णु देवता। ॐ विष्णवे नमः बीजम्। विष्णु शक्तिः। श्री विष्णु प्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Traditionally invoked to establish a diamond-like protective cage around the devotee
Traditionally recited for removal of enemies and grants victory in all endeavors
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins and bestows auspiciousness
Associated in tradition with diseases and ailments
Invokes all siddhis through mere remembrance
Provides protection from all ten directions
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Vishnu, Vishnu Panjara Stotram (विष्णु पञ्जर स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. Attributed to Narada Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to remove all sins and bestow auspiciousness and provide protection from all ten directions. It is also said to destroy enemies and grant victory in all endeavors. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Ekadashi. At 11 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 8 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Vishnu Panjara Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vishnu Panjara Stotram in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Diamond-cage protection prayer to Lord Vishnu for all-round safety.
relatedSearches: Vishnu Panjara Stotram lyrics Sanskrit translation
Twelve names of Vishnu Dwadasa Nama list
Eight directions Vishnu protection Kesava Narayana
Vishnu Panjara Stotram benefits daily protection
Vishnu twelve forms Kesava to Damodara

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the title Vishnu Panjara Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Panjara in Sanskrit means a cage or enclosure, and Vishnu Panjara Stotram is therefore the prayer of the unbreakable cage formed by the twelve names and weapons of Vishnu surrounding the devotee on every side. Each verse appoints one of the twelve forms of Vishnu (the Dwadasa Nama: Kesava, Narayana, Madhava, Govinda, Vishnu, Madhusudana, Trivikrama, Vamana, Sridhara, Hrishikesha, Padmanabha and Damodara) to guard a specific direction or limb, with the corresponding ayudha (weapon) raised in defence. The result is a complete envelope of protection within which the devotee dwells safe from danger. The variant title Vishnu Vajra Panjara Stotram emphasises the diamond-hard (vajra) impenetrability of this cage. (Sources: Hindupedia Vishnu Panjara stotram; Stotra Nidhi Vishnu Vajra Panjara; Kamakoti Vishnu Panjara Stotra.)

seoFaqs.question: Which Purana is the source of Vishnu Panjara Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Two Puranic sources are commonly cited and represent two slightly different recensions. The Garuda Purana version is the most widely circulated and is associated with funerary and protective rites, since the Garuda Purana itself is read at the time of death. The Brahmanda Purana version, published as Sri Vishnu Vajra Panjara Stotram, includes additional verses elaborating the directional protection scheme and the twelve weapons. Both versions share the core Dwadasa Nama and the ashta-dik directional protection structure. The Garuda Purana recension is preferred in Vaishnava household practice; the Brahmanda Purana recension is preferred in tantra-influenced kavacha collections. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Brahmanda Purana version; Hindupedia; Raja Thatha translations.)

seoFaqs.question: How are the twelve directions and forms arranged?
seoFaqs.answer: The standard arrangement names Vishnu's forms guarding the cardinal and intercardinal directions and the six axes (above, below, in front, behind, on the right and on the left). Kesava in the east holding the wheel of molten gold, Narayana in the west holding the conch and resembling a rich blue cloud, Madhava on top holding a mace, Govinda in the south holding an arrow, and so on through the twelve forms. The remaining axes are guarded by Vishnu, Madhusudana, Trivikrama, Vamana, Sridhara, Hrishikesha, Padmanabha and Damodara, each with their characteristic weapon. The twelve names also map to the twelve months of the year and the twelve solar months in some commentaries, expanding the protective scope across time as well as space. (Sources: Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi; Raja Thatha Dwadasa Nama Panjara.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Vishnu Panjara Stotram promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti at the end of the stotram states that those who read the prayer according to the prescribed rules and understand its meaning are freed from all daily dangers, all sins of the past are absolved, and at the end of life they reach Vishnu-loka. Additional benefits enumerated in commentaries include protection from snake bites and ghost possession, security during travel through unfamiliar territory, safety of children and pregnant women in the household, neutralisation of black-magic attempts, recovery from prolonged illness, and the dissolution of fear arising from political or financial uncertainty. The hymn is also recommended as a deathbed recitation for ushering the soul to Vaikuntha. (Sources: Hindupedia; Kamakoti Vishnu Panjara Stotra; Celextel Vishnu Panjara.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Vishnu Panjara Stotram be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Daily recitation is recommended in the morning after bath and before leaving the home, since the protective cage is held to remain in force for the day's activities. A second recitation at sunset before the night's rest is also common, since the night is considered a vulnerable time for unseen attacks. For special protection during illness, travel or perceived threat, the stotram is recited 11, 21 or 108 times. Devotees facing extended difficulty undertake a 41-day anushthana, reciting the hymn 108 times per day. The recitation is done facing east in the morning and north at night, with a Tulsi mala in hand and a small Sudarshana yantra or Vishnu picture before the practitioner. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Celextel; Kamakoti.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Vishnu Panjara Stotram compare to other Vaishnava kavachas?
seoFaqs.answer: Within the Vaishnava kavacha tradition, Vishnu Panjara Stotram is one of three principal protective compositions. The Sudarshana Ashtakam by Vedanta Desika focuses entirely on the Sudarshana chakra as a single weapon-deity sealing the devotee's environment, particularly potent against tantric attacks. The Narasimha Kavacham from the Brahmanda Purana invokes Narasimha to guard each limb against fear, particularly potent at night and against ghost possession. Vishnu Panjara Stotram differs by deploying twelve forms simultaneously across all directions, providing multidirectional rather than focused single-weapon protection. Many devotees combine all three: Vishnu Panjara in the morning, Sudarshana at noon, and Narasimha Kavacham at night. (Sources: Hindupedia; Celextel; Stotra Nidhi.)
